questions.question: What primary feature does Notion provide for code snippets?

options.explanation: Notion highlights code according to its language, making it easier to read.

options.text: Automatic translation of code to other languages
options.explanation: Notion does not translate code; it only formats it.
Spell checking is not relevant to code blocks and isn’t a featured function.
Code blocks are for display only; they cannot run code inside Notion.

questions.question: Approximately how many programming languages does Notion support for syntax highlighting?

options.explanation: The article states Notion provides automatic syntax highlighting for over 60 languages.
The number is higher than 30; the article mentions over 60.
While Notion supports many languages, the article specifies “over 60,” not exactly 100.
Ten is far fewer than the number actually supported.

questions.question: Which option can be adjusted to improve the visibility of a code block in Notion?

options.explanation: Wrapping code prevents horizontal scrolling and makes long lines easier to read.

options.text: Change the background color to match the page
options.explanation: Background color changes are not mentioned as a built‑in visibility option.
Hiding line numbers would reduce visibility, not improve it.

options.text: Embed a video inside the code block
options.explanation: Videos cannot be embedded inside a code block; they are separate media.

questions.question: Where can users find templates that include code blocks?

options.explanation: The article directs users to browse over 10,000 templates in the template gallery.
While the Help Center explains code blocks, templates are located in the gallery.
YouTube hosts videos, not ready‑to‑use Notion templates.
External blogs may have examples, but the article points to Notion’s own gallery.

questions.question: What is a key benefit of using code blocks for engineers, as highlighted in the article?

options.text: Better documentation and more efficient work
options.explanation: The article states that code blocks lead to better documentation and help engineers work more efficiently.
Code blocks have no impact on network performance.

options.text: Automatic execution of scripts within Notion
options.explanation: Notion only displays code; it does not run scripts.

options.text: Built‑in debugging tools for every language
options.explanation: Debugging tools are not part of Notion’s code block feature.

questions.question: Which of the following features is NOT mentioned as part of Notion’s code block capabilities?

options.explanation: The article explicitly mentions a copy‑to‑clipboard option.
Syntax highlighting is highlighted as a core feature.

options.text: Real‑time collaboration on the code block
options.explanation: While Notion supports collaborative editing of pages, the article does not list real‑time collaboration as a specific code‑block feature.

options.text: Option to wrap code for better visibility
options.explanation: Wrapping code is mentioned as a visibility adjustment.

questions.question: The article notes that an ad blocker prevented a video from playing. What does this imply about Notion’s integration of external media, and how might it affect a user’s experience when accessing help resources?

options.text: Embedded external videos can be blocked by ad‑blocking extensions, so users may need to open the video on an external platform like YouTube to view it.
options.explanation: The message indicates the video is embedded from an external source; ad blockers can interfere, requiring users to follow the provided YouTube link.

options.text: Notion stores all help videos locally, so ad blockers have no effect on playback.
options.explanation: The issue described shows that the video is not stored locally; it is an external embed.

options.text: Ad blockers enhance video quality by removing ads, which is why the video failed to load.
options.explanation: Ad blockers block content, not improve quality; the failure is due to blocking, not enhancement.

options.text: Notion automatically disables all external media when an ad blocker is detected, preventing any help content from being displayed.
options.explanation: Only the specific video was blocked; the rest of the article remains accessible.
